title: Solid stores + Tauri bridge layer
analysis:
Task 85 creates the reactive state management layer and IPC bridge for the Reify GUI frontend. Task 81 (scaffold) is a dependency but the gui/ directory does not exist on any branch or worktree (checked main and task/82-85, all at same commit with no gui/), so this task must bootstrap the minimal SolidJS project scaffold as a prerequisite.

The IPC types to mirror are defined in the task description: MeshData, ValueData, ConstraintData, SourceLocation, FileData, GuiState, plus EvaluationStatus. The Rust-side types in reify-types (Value enum in value.rs, Mesh struct in geometry.rs with vertices: Vec<f32>/indices: Vec<u32>/normals: Option<Vec<f32>>, Constraint types in constraint.rs, SourceSpan/Diagnostic in diagnostics.rs) inform the TypeScript shape but the IPC types are serialized/simplified versions.

The bridge layer wraps Tauri invoke() and listen() APIs with typed functions. Since Tauri won't be available in test environments, the bridge is mocked via vi.mock().

Three Solid stores (engine, selection, editor) use createStore from solid-js/store. Tests use Vitest with jsdom and SolidJS createRoot for reactive test isolation. The engine store subscribes to Tauri events via bridge, tested by mocking bridge event listeners.

Key design: Factory functions (createXStore) for test isolation. Record<string, T> for store collections (Solid proxies don't work with Map/Set). string[] for dirtyFiles (same reason).

prerequisites.description: Create minimal gui/ project scaffold: package.json with solid-js, @tauri-apps/api (^2), vitest, @solidjs/testing-library, jsdom, vite, vite-plugin-solid, typescript. tsconfig.json with strict mode, JSX preserve for solid-js. vite.config.ts with solid plugin. vitest.config.ts with jsdom environment. index.html entry point. Minimal src/index.tsx and src/App.tsx. Run pnpm install. Add gui/node_modules to .gitignore.

steps.description: Create gui/src/__tests__/types.typecheck.ts: compile-time type check importing all types from types.ts. Creates typed variables asserting: MeshData with entityPath/vertices/indices/normals, ValueData with cellId/entityPath/memberName/value/determinacy, ConstraintData with constraintId/entityPath/satisfaction/messages, SourceLocation with filePath/line/column, FileData with path/content/language, GuiState with meshes/values/constraints/files/evalStatus, EvaluationStatus with phase union and optional progress, type aliases MeshUpdate/ValueUpdate/ConstraintUpdate. Won't compile since types.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/types.ts with all IPC types: MeshData (entityPath: string, vertices: Float32Array, indices: Uint32Array, normals: Float32Array | null), ValueData (cellId/entityPath/memberName/value/determinacy: string), ConstraintData (constraintId/entityPath: string, satisfaction: 'satisfied'|'violated'|'indeterminate', messages: string[]), SourceLocation (filePath: string, line/column: number), FileData (path/content/language: string), GuiState (meshes/values/constraints/files arrays + evalStatus), EvaluationStatus (phase: 'idle'|'evaluating'|'resolving', progress?: number), type aliases MeshUpdate/ValueUpdate/ConstraintUpdate. Run tsc --noEmit.

Create gui/src/__tests__/bridge.test.ts: mock @tauri-apps/api/core invoke() and @tauri-apps/api/event listen() via vi.mock(). Tests: (1) getInitialState calls invoke('get_initial_state'). (2) setParameter calls invoke('set_parameter', {cellId, value}). (3) updateSource calls invoke('update_source', {filePath, content}). (4) onMeshUpdate calls listen('mesh-update', cb) returns unlisten. (5) onEvaluationStatus calls listen('evaluation-status', cb). Fails because bridge.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/bridge.ts: 9 command functions (getInitialState, setParameter, updateSource, saveFile, openFile, exportGeometry, getSourceLocation, focusEntity, lspRequest) wrapping invoke(). 6 event listeners (onMeshUpdate, onValueUpdate, onConstraintUpdate, onEvaluationStatus, onDiagnostics, onFileChanged) wrapping listen() extracting event.payload. Returns Promise<UnlistenFn>. All bridge tests pass.

Create gui/src/__tests__/selectionStore.test.ts in createRoot: (1) initial null selectedEntity/hoveredEntity. (2) selectEntity('Bracket') sets selectedEntity. (3) selectEntity(null) clears. (4) hoverEntity sets/clears. (5) select/hover independent. Fails because selectionStore.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/stores/selectionStore.ts: createSelectionStore() factory returning { state, selectEntity, hoverEntity }. State: { selectedEntity: string | null, hoveredEntity: string | null }. Uses Solid createStore. All selection tests pass.

Create gui/src/__tests__/editorStore.test.ts in createRoot: (1) initial empty state. (2) openFile adds FileData, sets activeFile. (3) duplicate open doesn't add twice. (4) closeFile removes, handles activeFile switch. (5) closeFile removes from dirtyFiles. (6) markDirty/markClean toggles. (7) setActiveFile. (8) setCursorPosition. Fails because editorStore.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/stores/editorStore.ts: createEditorStore() factory returning { state, openFile, closeFile, setActiveFile, markDirty, markClean, setCursorPosition }. State: { openFiles: FileData[], activeFile: string | null, dirtyFiles: string[], cursorPosition: {line: number, column: number} | null }. All editor tests pass.

Create gui/src/__tests__/engineStore.test.ts in createRoot: (1) initial empty state, evalStatus {phase:'idle'}. (2) initFromState populates from GuiState. (3) applyMeshUpdate upserts by entityPath. (4) applyValueUpdates batch-upserts by cellId. (5) applyConstraintUpdates batch-upserts. (6) setEvalStatus updates. (7) subscribeToEvents wires bridge listeners, returns cleanup. Fails because engineStore.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/stores/engineStore.ts: createEngineStore() factory returning { state, initFromState, applyMeshUpdate, applyValueUpdates, applyConstraintUpdates, setEvalStatus, subscribeToEvents }. State: { meshes: Record<string, MeshData>, values: Record<string, ValueData>, constraints: Record<string, ConstraintData>, evalStatus: EvaluationStatus }. subscribeToEvents wires bridge listeners, returns cleanup fn. All engine tests pass.
Run full test suite (vitest run) and type-check (tsc --noEmit). Verify all tests pass and TypeScript compiles cleanly.
Create gui/src/stores/index.ts barrel re-exporting all three store factories. Final cleanup and verification.

design_decisions.decision: Create minimal scaffold as prerequisite since Task 81 gui/ doesn't exist
design_decisions.rationale: Task 81 (scaffold) not completed: no gui/ directory on any branch. Prerequisites create the minimal scaffold needed. Full scaffold with Tauri cargo config, CSS, etc. can be layered on later.

design_decisions.decision: Vitest with jsdom for store tests
design_decisions.rationale: Standard for Vite/SolidJS projects. jsdom provides DOM APIs for SolidJS reactivity. Store tests use createRoot for reactive context isolation per test.
Tauri APIs only available inside Tauri webview. vi.mock() provides clean isolation for bridge tests.

design_decisions.decision: Record<string, T> for store collections, not Map
design_decisions.rationale: Solid's createStore Proxy works with plain objects/arrays, not Map/Set. Record enables fine-grained per-key reactivity.
Solid createStore Proxy cannot track Set mutations. Array with includes/filter is compatible and sufficient for expected file counts.

design_decisions.decision: Factory function pattern (createXStore) for all stores
design_decisions.rationale: Singleton stores share state across tests causing flakiness. Factories enable fresh instances per test. In the app, stores created once and shared via context.
Binary array data from Rust. Typed arrays match WebGL expectations and enable efficient Tauri IPC transfer.
Tauri convention uses kebab-case ('mesh-update'). Bridge translates to camelCase JS names (onMeshUpdate) while using correct strings internally.

reuse.where: crates/reify-types/src/geometry.rs (Mesh), value.rs (Value, DeterminacyState, Satisfaction), constraint.rs (ConstraintResult), diagnostics.rs (Diagnostic, SourceSpan)
reuse.how: TS IPC types mirror serialized forms: MeshData mirrors Mesh, ValueData mirrors ValueCellId+Value, Satisfaction maps to string union.
All stores use createStore([initial]) returning [state, setState]. Actions use setState with path-based updates.
Bridge wraps invoke<T>(cmd, args) for commands, listen<T>(event, handler) for events. UnlistenFn for cleanup.

reuse.where: All reify-* crates follow test-first pattern
reuse.how: Same discipline for TypeScript: test files first, each step atomic. Factory pattern for test isolation mirrors Rust per-test setup.
